title: LEFT JOIN
content:
### LEFT JOIN (LEFT OUTER JOIN)

LEFT JOIN returns ALL rows from the left table, and matching rows from the right table. If there is no match, NULL values are returned for right table columns.

---

### LEFT JOIN Syntax

```sql
SELECT columns
FROM table1
LEFT JOIN table2 ON table1.column = table2.column;
```

### LEFT JOIN Example

```sql
-- All employees, with department name if they have one
SELECT e.name, d.dept_name
FROM employees e
LEFT JOIN departments d ON e.dept_id = d.id;

-- Employees without a department will show NULL for dept_name
```

### Finding Unmatched Rows

```sql
-- Find employees without a department
SELECT e.name
FROM employees e
LEFT JOIN departments d ON e.dept_id = d.id
WHERE d.id IS NULL;
```

### LEFT JOIN with Multiple Tables

```sql
SELECT e.name, d.dept_name, p.project_name
FROM employees e
LEFT JOIN departments d ON e.dept_id = d.id
LEFT JOIN project_assignments pa ON e.id = pa.employee_id
LEFT JOIN projects p ON pa.project_id = p.id;
```

### Comparing LEFT JOIN and INNER JOIN

| Employee | INNER JOIN | LEFT JOIN |
|----------|-----------|-----------|
| Has department | Included | Included |
| No department | Excluded | Included (NULL for dept) |

> **Key Point:** LEFT JOIN is useful when you need all rows from the primary table, regardless of whether related data exists in the secondary table.
codeExample:
SELECT c.name AS customer_name,
       COUNT(o.id) AS order_count
FROM customers c
LEFT JOIN orders o ON c.id = o.customer_id
GROUP BY c.name
ORDER BY order_count DESC;

quiz.question: How many rows does LEFT JOIN return from the left table?
What appears in right-table columns when there is no match in a LEFT JOIN?
